skill.name: notion-api
skill.description: Use the Notion REST API to read, create, update, and delete workspace content with documented curl and JSON request patterns.
skill.summary: Interact with Notion pages, databases, blocks, users, comments, filters, sorts, and rich text through REST API calls.
skill.author: intellectronica

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and blocker alerts, but review found they are mostly Markdown code fences, inline code, supported language names, and JSON examples rather than executable skill code. Real risk remains medium because the skill instructs agents to use curl against the Notion API, handle bearer tokens, and perform destructive Notion operations after user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Notion API Network Access
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally sends REST requests to https://api.notion.com using curl examples. This is required for the skill, but it can expose workspace data if used with an over-permissive integration token.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show direct curl calls to the Notion API. The destination is the expected service for this skill, so the risk is legitimate but not suspicious.

medium_findings.title: Bearer Token Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill reads or requests a Notion API token and places it in Authorization headers. It also instructs agents not to display, log, or send the token anywhere else.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token handling behavior is explicitly documented. The same section includes protective handling guidance, reducing concern about malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: User-Authorized Destructive Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill documents update, delete, archive, and schema modification workflows for Notion. It requires user confirmation before destructive operations, but these actions can still alter workspace data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive-operation policy and delete endpoint examples are present in the skill. Confirmation guidance makes this an operational risk rather than evidence of abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Alerts Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many inline backticks and fenced examples as Ruby shell execution. Review found documentation examples for curl, jq, JSON, supported code languages, and Notion request bodies, not executable bundled code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown text or code examples. No executable Ruby backtick invocation was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts map to ordinary prose, sorting examples, annotation fields, and empty rich text examples. No hashing or cryptographic implementation evidence was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description text or Notion JSON examples. The review did not find MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: PowerShell Alert Is a Supported Language Name
low_findings.description: The PowerShell finding appears in a list of Notion code block language values. It is not an instruction to invoke PowerShell or execute a script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a Notion API language enumeration. It has no command invocation semantics.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell-Based REST Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses curl and jq command examples for API interaction. Users should run these only with trusted inputs and scoped Notion integration tokens.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in shell code fences. It is expected for this API skill but still deserves marketplace risk disclosure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Use In Authorization Headers
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill places Notion tokens into Authorization headers. The documented destination is api.notion.com and the skill warns against exposing tokens elsewhere.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential use is clear and intentional. The protective guidance and expected Notion endpoint reduce the likelihood of malicious exfiltration.

content.user_title: Automate Notion with REST API Calls
content.value_statement: Working with Notion data by hand is slow and error prone. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical REST patterns for safe Notion automation.
actual_capabilities: Build Notion REST requests for pages, databases, blocks, users, and comments.
Apply documented filters, sorts, pagination, and property retrieval patterns.
Create and update page content with Notion block and rich text structures.
Handle authentication headers with a Notion integration token.
Plan destructive operations with explicit user confirmation steps.
Interpret common Notion API errors and rate limit responses.
limitations: Requires a valid Notion integration token with access to the target workspace content.
Uses REST API examples and does not include a packaged client library.
Cannot access pages or databases that are not shared with the integration.
Destructive operations depend on user approval and correct target identifiers.

use_cases.title: Build a Team Knowledge Sync
use_cases.description: Create or update Notion pages from structured team notes, release plans, or meeting summaries.

use_cases.title: Query Product Databases
use_cases.description: Filter and sort roadmap, feedback, or task databases using Notion API query patterns.

use_cases.title: Maintain Developer Documentation
use_cases.description: Add blocks, rich text, code samples, and comments to engineering documentation pages.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Notion Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Notion API skill to verify that my integration token can access Notion. Do not print the token.

prompt_templates.title: Find Matching Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Notion for pages matching "{topic}". Show the request plan first, then retrieve the most relevant page metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Structured Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a Notion API request to create a page under {parent_page_id} with headings, paragraphs, and a task list. Ask before sending it.

prompt_templates.title: Automate a Database Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Query database {database_id} for items where Status is "Ready" and Due Date is this week. Propose safe updates and request confirmation before changing anything.

output_examples.input: Find all open tasks due this week in my Notion project database.
output_examples.output: A concise query plan, the matching task list, and pagination notes if more results are available.

output_examples.input: Create a meeting notes page with agenda, decisions, and action items.
output_examples.output: A confirmation request followed by a structured Notion page creation plan using headings, paragraphs, and checklist blocks.

output_examples.input: Explain why my Notion request returned a validation error.
output_examples.output: A plain language diagnosis of the likely field mismatch and a corrected request structure.

best_practices: Use the least privileged Notion integration token that can complete the task.
Confirm page IDs, database IDs, and destructive changes before sending write requests.
Handle pagination, rate limits, and validation errors in every workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not paste or print Notion API tokens in chat output, logs, or page content.
Do not run bulk updates or deletes without reviewing the exact affected records.
Do not assume visible workspace pages are accessible unless they are shared with the integration.

faq.question: Does this skill include a Notion client library?
faq.answer: No. It provides REST API instructions, request patterns, and reference material for direct API calls.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it update or delete Notion content?
faq.answer: Yes, when the token has permission. The skill instructs agents to ask for confirmation before destructive changes.

faq.question: How should I provide the Notion API token?
faq.answer: Use an environment variable when possible. If you provide a token in context, the agent should not display it.

faq.question: Can it work with private pages?
faq.answer: Only if the Notion integration has been granted access to those pages or databases.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses network requests, bearer tokens, and write operations. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection.
